title: Encoder-Decoder Summarizers
concept: Models like BART or T5 encode the long document, then decode a condensed summary autoregressively, using cross-attention to stay accurate.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of an encoder-decoder model like BART or T5?

options.text: To read a long text and write a short summary of it
To calculate math formulas really fast
To store files on a hard drive

exercises.explain: Encoder-Decoder models are designed to read one sequence (like a long article) and generate a new sequence (like a short summary).

cards.title: Super Summarizers!
cards.body: Have you ever had to read a massive school chapter and write a quick summary of it? It's tough to keep it short without losing the key facts! Modern AI models use a two-step trick—encoding and decoding—to write perfect summaries every time.

cards.title: What is an Encoder-Decoder?
cards.body: Encoder-Decoder models (like BART and T5) are AI architectures built for summarizing text. The Encoder reads and understands the long input document, while the Decoder writes a brand-new, shortened summary. They stay accurate by using 'cross-attention' to look back at the original text while writing.

cards.title: The Bilingual Translator
cards.body: Think of an encoder-decoder summarizer like a translator at a global summit. First, the translator listens carefully and takes notes on a long speech (Encoding). Then, they write down a neat, short summary in their own words (Decoding), constantly glancing back at their notes to make sure they don't miss any facts.

cards.title: Summarizing Space News
cards.body: Imagine feeding a 10-page news article about a space launch into BART. The encoder digests all 10 pages. The decoder then writes: 'Yesterday, NASA successfully launched its new rocket to the moon.' It didn't just copy-paste a sentence; it generated a fresh summary.

cards.title: Myth: Just Copy-Pasting?
cards.body: Many people think AI summarizers just copy and paste the most important sentences from the original text. In reality, encoder-decoder models write completely new sentences from scratch, just like a human student would!

cards.title: Encoder vs. Decoder
cards.body: Let's compare the two halves of the summarizer system and see how they collaborate.

left.text: Reads and digests the entire long input document to understand its meaning.

right.text: Generates a new, condensed summary word by word, referring to the encoder's notes.

exercises.prompt: What mechanism does the decoder use to stay accurate and look back at the original text?
exercises.explain: Cross-attention allows the decoder to look back at the encoder's representation of the source document, ensuring the generated summary remains factually accurate.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Encoder-decoder summarizers only copy-paste exact sentences from the input text.
exercises.explain: Encoder-Decoder models generate a brand-new summary (abstractive) rather than just extracting and copying sentences directly.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing part of the summarization pipeline.
exercises.sentence: The encoder processes the original document, and the _______ writes the summary.
exercises.explain: The encoder reads and represents the source document, while the decoder is responsible for generating the summary word-by-word.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI assistants is using a true Encoder-Decoder model to summarize a document?

options.text: BART, which encodes the full report and decodes a fresh summary.
GPT-2, which only predicts the next word without an encoder.
BERT, which only highlights existing words in the text.

exercises.explain: BART is an encoder-decoder model specifically designed for text generation tasks like summarization, unlike BERT (encoder-only) or GPT-2 (decoder-only).

exercises.prompt: How does an encoder-decoder model like T5 or BART successfully summarize a long document?

options.text: It randomly deletes 80% of the words to make the text shorter.
It only reads the first and last sentences of the document.
The encoder reads the text, and the decoder writes a new summary using cross-attention to check the source.
It searches for the most common words and lists them in order.

exercises.explain: Encoder-decoder models are powerful because they don't just crop existing sentences. The encoder processes the full source text, and the decoder generates new summary words one by one, using cross-attention to look back at the encoder's work to keep facts straight.
